a:r.a:t: Important notice (2/2)
Benchmarking
Include if separate benchmarking analysis is carried out

The benchmarking analysis is based on information obtained from comparable industries and/or activities. As with any benchmarking analysis there is a risk that the information is not fully comparable. If the benchmarked information is based on aggregated industry information, access to more detailed information could have led to the analyses being presented in another manner or to different data being presented. We accept no responsibility for such differences.

The benchmarking analysis presented in this report presents aggregated or company level data collected from third-party data sources as referenced. We accept no responsibility for the correctness of the data obtained from third-party sources.

Benchmarking results should be treated as indicative, as benchmarking results will vary based on the industry segmentation, geography selected, and periodicity. While scoring lower or higher than peers on a select
key performance indicators (“KPIs”)
may indicate underperformance or superior performance, underlying factors must be understood, including the interaction between different KPIs.
Value creation potential
Include if VC work is carried out

During the course of our work, we have attempted to identify potential value creation opportunities to facilitate discussions on areas of potential value enhancement. The value enhancement opportunities shown are indicative estimates only and are based on limited information, high level analysis and assumptions. The opportunity analysis have been carried out using limited data and do not comprise a detailed diagnostic. Accordingly, the analysis needs to be interpreted with caution. The assumptions used in the calculation of base case/stretch case upside potential are set forth within our analysis.

KPMG gives no assurance over the achievement of any value enhancement opportunities included in the report. In addition, since the value creation opportunities and the assumptions on which they are based relate to the future they may also be affected by unforeseen events and circumstances. Achievement of any value creation opportunity will also be dependent on the implementation of action plans and the steps taken by management.

The benchmarking information used in the value creation analysis is based upon publicly available information - there can be no guarantee that such information is accurate as of the date it was obtained or that it will continue to be accurate in the future. KPMG have relied upon and assumed, without independent verification, the accuracy and completeness of information available from public sources.

The value enhancement opportunities shown within are not intended to fully capture all the inter-relationships that exist between the individual opportunity areas. As a result, it may not be possible to realize some or all of the opportunities identified. Any actions that you choose to take as a result of the directional views provided, are the responsibility of the management team. KPMG will not use the information presented to make any decisions or judgement on behalf of management. Such decisions are the responsibility of the management team who must designate an individual who possesses the suitable skill, knowledge and experience to be responsible at all times for these decisions.

In performing the value creation potential analysis, we have also recommended certain "Next steps to validate opportunity". We are happy to discuss how we can support you with these steps, subject to contract.
Pre-Deal PPA benchmarking – KPMG database
Include if separate PPA benchmarking analysis is carried out

The database consists of PPAs carried out by KPMG globally and information from the public domain; any conclusions therefore drawn using the database are however limited due to having directly comparable data (e.g. by operations, market, and size).
The benchmarking analysis set out within this report is based on information on comparable industries, companies and/or activities obtained from:

publicly available information - there can be no guarantee that such information is accurate as of the date it was obtained or that it will continue to be accurate in the future. KPMG has relied upon and assumed, without independent verification, the accuracy and completeness of information available from public sources. We accept no responsibility for the correctness of the data obtained from public or third-party sources;

KPMG proprietary benchmarking data. Where data is sourced from KPMG’s proprietary benchmarking database, it is based on aggregated data and includes at least [XX] entities, and the metrics are calculated as an average.

As with any benchmarking analysis there is a risk that the information utilized is not fully comparable. Where the benchmarking analysis is based on aggregated information, it is also the case that access to more detailed information could have led to the analysis being presented in a different manner or to different data being presented. In addition, such analysis will vary based on factors such as the industry segmentation, the selected geography selected, and periodicity. The benchmarking analysis should therefore be treated with caution and as directional and indicative only. Further, detailed work would be needed to determine the identify and value the acquired intangibles.
Valuation (NPV) analysis
Include if VCM work is carried out

The indicative NPV impact of the potential value creation opportunities presented in this [report], is set out in the Value creation potential important notice. The NPVs of the value enhancement opportunities are calculated separately, with each value creation opportunity assessed on a standalone basis.
The NPVs of the value creations opportunities are indicative estimates only which are based on limited information, high-level analysis and assumptions.

KPMG gives no assurance over the achievement of any value enhancement opportunities included in the report. In addition, since the value creation opportunities and the assumptions on which they are based relate to the future they may also be affected by unforeseen events and circumstances. Achievement of any value creation opportunity will also be dependent on the implementation of action plans and the steps taken by management.
In particular: (
i

) we have not assessed the identified valuation creation opportunities independently, the implementation cost of realizing those opportunities or their timing; and (ii) we have not sought to independently assess the risks associated with the identified value creation opportunities but have relied on your internal assessment of the discount rate and risks inherent in the business as well as your terminal value growth rate.

To the extent any or all assumptions may differ this could have a material impact on the resulting NPV analysis. As such, this does not present KPMG’s independent view on the value of any or all identified impacts.

Our Report makes reference to “KPMG analysis”, this indicates only that we have (where specified) undertaken certain analytical activities on the underlying data to arrive at the information presented.
Executive summary
Historical trading
Forecast trading
Balance sheet
Cash flows
Reporting
Value creation
Pre-Deal PPA
Taxation
Appendices
